Railway company, and we hereby de-
mand of the members of the city
council representing the interests of
this city, that they reject said offer
as unjust to the city and to the prop
erty holders owning property along
said streets and avenue.


Resolved. further, that we are in
favor of requiring said streets and
avenue to be kept open, and that pub
lie travel over said crossings be prop
erly safeguarded by the enforcement
of existing ordinances with respect
to said crossings. and the adoption of
such additional ordinances as will
render said crossings safe and con-
venient for public use until such
time as the Northern Pacific Railway
company shall do what we believe to
be the only ultimate solution of the
difficulties confronting the operation
of said railway through this city, to-
wit, the lowering of the grade of the
railway through the city 50 as to per-
nit overhead crossings practically on
the grades of the streets"
